info.instructions:
• Task: Read the source text and competing translations. Highlight all errors. Rate each translation (each column is an output from a single model/translator). At the end, click Next in top-right corner.

  • Highlighting errors:
    • Select text containing an error to mark it as Minor.
    • Click selected text to change the error to Major or remove the highlight.
    • Missing text: Highlight the [MISSING] tag if the translation omits important source text.

  • Error severity:
    • Minor: Imperfections or stylistic issues that do not impact the core message (e.g., awkward phrasing).
    • Major: Confuses meaning, misrepresents the source, or violates the message (e.g., incorrect information, confusing wording).

  • Important rules:
    • Multiple errors: Use separate highlights for each error.
    • Hallucinations: Highlight unsupported extra text; mark as Major.
    • Wrong language: Highlight the entire text, mark as Major, and assign a score of 0.
    • Consistency: Check translation consistency (e.g., technical terms) across the document.

  • Rating scale (0-100%):
    • 85-100% (Very Good): Complete meaning transfer; perfectly natural; no or minimal proofreading.
    • 65-80% (Good): Near-complete transfer, minor inaccuracies; mostly natural, minor awkwardness; needs light proofreading.
    • 45-60% (Acceptable): Main ideas conveyed, noticeable inaccuracies or omissions; uneven naturalness, awkward phrasing; usable only after substantial revision.
    • 25-40% (Borderline): Partial transfer; frequent misinterpretation or omission confusing the message; often unnatural; requires major rewrite.
    • 0-20% (Not acceptable): Violation of meaning; large portions mistranslated, missing, or incoherent; unusable without complete retranslation.

  • For unexpected problems and remarks, use comment box in ⚙️ (top right).

payload.src: Meghan's rebranded herself - and it's another valiant attempt to flog poshness to the little people
payload.src_text: Meghan's rebranded herself - and it's another valiant attempt to flog poshness to the little people

tgt.Claude-4: メーガンが自身をリブランド化 - 庶民に上品さを売り込む勇敢な試みがまた一つ

payload.src: In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.
payload.src_text: In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.

tgt.Claude-4: この暗い時代において、我々は見つけられるところで喜びを見出さなければならない。そして信頼できる源の一つがサセックス公爵夫人である。最新の事業において、大きなプラットフォームを持ちながらそれを埋めるのに十分なものがないという難しい立場にあるメーガン（ブルックリン・ベッカムも同様）は、短いビデオの中で「私が大切にしているすべてのもの - 食べ物、ガーデニング、おもてなし、思慮深い生活、そして日常の中に喜びを見つけること」を「美しく織り合わせる」と約束した新しいライフスタイルブランドを立ち上げた。ブランド「アメリカン・リビエラ・オーチャード」は終わった。新ブランド「アズ・エバー」よ、永遠なれ。

payload.src: The duchess and her husband, Prince Harry, have to do something and good luck to them, but it's hard not to study the rollout of As Ever for signs of the widening gap between Meghan's self-image and how the rest of the world sees her. ""As ever,"" writes the duchess on Instagram, "means "as it's always been" or some even say "in the same way as always,"" the "some" in this sentence apparently referring to the dictionary definition of a two-word phrase that no one has ever had trouble understanding.
payload.src_text: The duchess and her husband, Prince Harry, have to do something and good luck to them, but it's hard not to study the rollout of As Ever for signs of the widening gap between Meghan's self-image and how the rest of the world sees her. ""As ever,"" writes the duchess on Instagram, "means "as it's always been" or some even say "in the same way as always,"" the "some" in this sentence apparently referring to the dictionary definition of a two-word phrase that no one has ever had trouble understanding.

tgt.Claude-4: 公爵夫人と夫のハリー王子は何かをしなければならず、彼らの幸運を祈るが、メーガンの自己イメージと世界の他の人々が彼女をどう見ているかの間の拡大するギャップの兆候を求めて「アズ・エバー」の展開を研究せずにはいられない。公爵夫人はインスタグラムで「『アズ・エバー』は『いつものように』という意味、または『いつもと同じように』と言う人もいる」と書いているが、この文の「人もいる」は明らかに、誰も理解に困ったことのない二語の句の辞書的定義を指している。

payload.src: It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."
payload.src_text: It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."

tgt.Claude-4: これはもちろんばかげており、元王室殿下が採用した「こんにちは庶民の皆さん」という調子も同様で、有名人の無名人に対する一般的な態度、すなわち存在の鍵を共有するかのように当たり前のことを述べる態度と一致している。それでも私は、インスタグラムの投稿とそれに付随するビデオで薄い内容を膨らませなければならないという厳しい挑戦に直面しているメーガンに同情を覚える。「これが私のしていることです」と彼女はカメラに向かって言う。「過去数年間、皆さんとそれを共有することができませんでしたが、今はできます。」ここでの「それ」とは何で、何が彼女の共有を妨げていたのか？（それは彼女の前のブランドと同様にジャムに関わるかもしれない。彼女は「限定版」の保存食の瓶をクリス・ジェンナー、ミンディ・カリング、トレイシー・エリス・ロスらに送ることでそのブランドを立ち上げた。彼らは新鮮なレモンの袋も受け取ったようだ。）とにかく、内容が何であれ、彼女は準備ができる前に「アズ・エバー」のニュースを共有することを余儀なくされた海外の「ささやき」について暗く示唆している - なぜなら「私から最初に聞いてほしかった」からだ。
